name: Frozen Lemon Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine sugar, cornstarch, and gelatin.
2. Add water and lemon or lime juice to the mixture.
3. Cook over medium heat, stirring until slightly thickened.
4. Stir in a few drops of food coloring (yellow or green) if desired.
5. Allow the mixture to cool completely.
6. Chill until partially set, about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
7. In a small bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
8. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the juice mixture.
9. In another small bowl, whip the whipping cream until stiff peaks form.
10. Gently fold the whipped cream into the juice mixture.
11. Securely wrap 3-inch waxed paper or foil collar around the rim of a 1-quart souffle dish.
12. Pour the mixture into the prepared dish.
13. Freeze for 6 hours or overnight.
14. Remove the collar before serving.
15. Garnish with whipped cream, candy lemon drops, and gum drop slivers if desired.
16. Return leftovers to the freezer.
